contemporaneous_record.observed_behavior: Under higher input density, associative state changed once per processed event and rapidly saturated at the upper boundary.
contemporaneous_record.contemporaneous_diagnosis: The v0.1 stress report identified event-density-dependent plastic update and noted that an actual tick- or episode-level update cadence was not implemented.
contemporaneous_record.historical_repair: The associative, habit, and narrative coefficients were reduced and the report oracle was split into recovery-phase and post-recovery observations.
contemporaneous_record.observed_side_effect: The coefficient reduction delayed saturation but did not separate event count from elapsed time, leaving the temporal mechanism unresolved.

candidate_principles.statement: Persistent change should distinguish elapsed-time flow from occurrence-triggered jumps.
predicted_repairs: Separates idle recovery from event-driven plastic update
Prevents transport-level event partitioning from multiplying a single semantic dose
prohibited_legal_behavior: Genuine repeated exposure with distinct occurrence or reexposure identity may still accumulate
Spacing may legitimately change the trajectory
candidate_principles.failure_condition: After controlling semantic dose and timestamps, transport partition alone still changes persistent state beyond tolerance.

competing_repairs: Apply a fixed per-tick plastic update budget without introducing continuous flow
Update persistent state only at declared episode boundaries
Use saturation-dependent learning rates while retaining event-count updates
legal_control_cases: Thirty distinct exposures may produce more change than one exposure
One past occurrence reaccessed now may produce current activation without rewriting the past occurrence time
An idle interval may change recovery state while leaving evidence and world ledgers untouched
retrospective_interpretation.minimal_counterexample: Deliver one semantic occurrence as one message and as thirty transport fragments with identical occurrence identity, total dose, source, and timestamps; v0.1 updates slow state once per fragment.

independent_defects_addressed: Backlog age treated as if it were current event age
discriminating_predictions: Burst and spaced exposure can diverge even when event count matches
No-event elapsed time can change flow state but not append-only ledgers
